chapters.title: Learning to own your story
chapters.text:
When you hear the name Jennifer Lopez, what is the first thing that pops into your head, fame, glitter, sparkly outfits, tumultuous love life, carefree music — maybe something along those lines? While those things are an inherent part of who she is, they are, of course, not the whole truth. Like any of us, to get to where she is, Jennifer Lopez had to overcome many hurdles and work day and night to become better. Who she is on stage and who she is when she’s alone with her children are two very different and very similar people at the same time. And you know what? Both are great and worth getting to know!

This summary focuses on 2011 and 2012 — the two years of Lopez’s life that transformed her approach to work, life, and love. From a heartbreaking divorce to the extraordinarily successful world tour, Jennifer Lopez experienced both the worst and the best moments of her life during these two years. While her triumphs were always widely celebrated in the public eye, her more personal struggles always remained behind the scenes of the Jennifer Lopez show. She had to go through them alone.

She believed in putting only the positive side of her experiences into the world, always wanting to create an inspiring and carefree image. But running from the truth in such a manner can be very damaging to one’s mental health. Thankfully, before it was irreversibly too late, she realized that she, first and foremost, had to own her story in order to be all those things that she was trying to project to the world. When she finally found the courage to do that, she discovered the true meaning of luminous happiness.

Owning our story can be hard but not nearly as difficult as spending our lives running from it. Embracing our vulnerabilities is risky but not nearly as dangerous as giving up on love and belonging and joy—the experiences that make us the most vulnerable. Only when we are brave enough to explore the darkness will we discover the infinite power of our light. ~ Brené Brown

The process of creating this book was not an easy one because it involved reliving many dark memories and unpleasant experiences. Despite this, from the get-go, Jennifer Lopez knew that she absolutely had to finish it in order to share the life-changing insights that she gained on her life journey. The idea behind this book is a simple one — to help you learn important truths about surviving whatever life throws at you and picking yourself up after you hit rock bottom.

Learning through somebody else’s experiences will help you avoid learning those same harsh truths the hard way.

chapters.title: After you hit rock bottom, the only way is up
chapters.text:
There are moments in your life when everything seems hopeless and utterly terrible, and the bad stuff just seems to be piling on, making you forget all the good things that you’ve got going for you. Moments like these can only be described as hitting rock bottom. They hit you hard and are very difficult to recover from. It takes incredible strength to look past these bad things and separate yourself from the people and situations bringing you down.

For Jennifer Lopez, hitting rock bottom was sudden and shocking. Here she was, doing a beautiful photoshoot for L’Oreal, seemingly living every woman’s dream in all aspects of her life, when a terrible panic attack happened. The unexplained, paralyzing fear that she felt that day was her body’s way of saying: “Something’s wrong! You have to do something.”

Sudden panic attacks can be the signs of deep unresolved underlying issues that are hurting us, even if we are unaware of them.

All the things she pushed down and hid from were finally manifesting themselves, and it was terrifying. At that moment, she accepted the first in a series of hard-hitting truths — she didn’t want to be with her husband, Marc, anymore. Even though it was scary to admit, she felt the panic and anxiety simmer down a bit in doing so. This is the very nature of the truth — it always comes to the surface, even if you refuse to face it.

The end of yet another marriage felt like the end of the world. Not only did it mean personal heartbreak, but it also meant being judged by the whole world — the never-ending questions, manipulative headlines, and constant speculations. Perhaps that is why she tried so hard to ignore the ongoing problems in her and Marc’s relationship and keep their family from breaking up. In the end, she made the only possible right decision by realizing that divorce, though painful and complicated, would benefit her and her two children in the long run. Growing up in an unhappy household with two parents who argued all the time was not something she wanted her children to experience. In many ways, divorce was the only way to save their family.

The decision to leave her husband made Lopez reevaluate every aspect of her life and rethink her priorities. This seemingly negative thing, the situation that felt and hurt like hell, set in motion many transformative changes that would help her become the happiest she has ever been.

Accepting the unwanted truths is the only way to move forward in your life.

chapters.title: It’s your life. You should call the shots
chapters.text:
As children, we all have unique aspirations and dreams. We grow up with them and with our own views, thoughts, and ideas. Then, step by step, adulting deforms all those things that make us special. Often, if we are not mindful of this, our circumstances and everything around us will force us into the necessary molds. They make us adapt and give up our dreams and ideals to survive and fit the world around us. In doing so, we can lose our authenticity, become someone that others expect us to be. But without living our truths, we cannot be truly happy.

Coming out of her marriage, Jennifer Lopez slowly began to notice the patterns of her decisions and choices — though seemingly beneficial, none of them were truly hers. There was always someone dictating what she was supposed to do and who she was supposed to be — managers, producers, social media, journalists, stylists, and others. Most of them, of course, had the very best of intentions, but that didn’t change the fact that she wasn’t listening to herself, which was another major cause of her deep unhappiness. By following the ideas of others, she ended up caring more about how they perceived her than about what was right for her. It was the worst thing possible for an artist — measuring yourself by other people’s standards and not your own.

An authentic and truly successful individual is only ever in competition with one person — themselves.

After she realized how much of her “self” got lost in translation, how different the woman in the tabloids was from what she was like deep inside, she did the best possible thing — finally listened to herself. Nobody can ever know what’s good for you better than you, so trust your instincts and follow your heart — it will take you exactly where you need to be.

Your power is in your individuality, in being exactly who you are. No two artists are alike, just like no two people are alike. ~ Jennifer Lopez

chapters.title: Taking risks and facing fears always feels rewarding
chapters.text:
Different people react differently when everything seems to be falling apart and things don’t go their way. Some get depressed, some become aggressive, and for some become, well, a bit crazy! Jennifer Lopez thinks she definitely belongs to the latter. Whenever she faces difficulties, doubts, or scary changes, she gets crazy ideas and clings to them. These challenges are her way of getting distracted, of taking matters into her own hands, at least in some small ways.

For example, after having kids (an impossibly significant change!) Lopez decided to do a triathlon. This new exciting, and, without a doubt, scary experience helped her regain confidence and sense of self. By facing one of her fears and taking a risk in one single area, she empowered herself to be bold and decisive in other facets of her life.

Taking control of minor things in your life can boost your confidence and make dealing with major issues easier and less stressful.

After the divorce, she was in a similar situation — lost, exhausted, and at crossroads. She had to find something to put her on the right track and make her feel truly alive again. That’s when another crazy idea appeared — out of nowhere, Lopez called her manager and determinedly stated that she finally wanted to do a tour. And this is how her first and most successful series of live shows was born — out of a crazy bold idea during a time when everything felt scary and hopeless.

Her 2012 tour was not only about music. It was about her story, struggles, love, and undying belief that we can dance and love and be happy again no matter what life throws at us.

The process of creating the perfect show was stressful and complicated but insanely rewarding. The ultimate challenge was to prove herself as a singer, dancer, and artist, all while trying to figure out the intricacies of being a single mother. She created a perfect playlist that portrayed her journey and bared her soul to the audience.

As crazy and terrifying as it was, this idea was exactly the pick-me-up she needed. It was a risk worth taking, and she gladly reaped the rewards.

chapters.title: Remembering where you came from can help you along the way
chapters.text:
Wherever life takes you and whoever you become, always come back to the kid you once were and the places you once called home. Our early experiences often are the most influential and impactful — they shape our future lives. For this very reason, we must accept and explore them, not just run away.

For Jennifer Lopez, one of the most defining factors was the place where she grew up. Despite the glamour of Hollywood and her fame, at her very core, she always remained an ambitious girl from the Bronx in New York, Jenny from the block. The experiences of growing up in the Bronx are at the very heart of her passions, as this was where she first heard the music that inspired her, where she fell in love with dancing, and where she learned the beauty of diversity. The tumultuous liveliness of the Bronx gave birth to her colorful and energetic personality, while its noisiness became the soundtrack of her life.

When you are young and unafraid, you see endless possibilities, even in the smallest things. Everything seems to be within your reach — attainable, simple. This feeling is what you should always return to whenever you’re struggling or lack motivation. It will help you get back on track. Memories of her past are what invariably kept Jennifer Lopez grounded. Despite her fame and success, she never lost touch with the girl she was before all the glitz and glamour — a hard worker, struggling for a chance to make it. She got to where she is not by luck but by following the example of her parents and putting in hours upon hours of diligent work and training.

To get to the top, you must put your head down and work towards your goal. Never rely on luck alone.

chapters.title: Before loving others, you must learn to love yourself
chapters.text:
Love has always been the most important part of Jennifer Lopez’s life. It has been a constant theme in her music and in everything she did. She fought for her relationships, was always reluctant to give up, and often let her feelings define her.

Love has always been my message. Love in all its glory, the good and the bad — wanting to get it right, always wanting, always hoping, always fighting. ~ Jennifer Lopez

She had quite a few serious relationships throughout her life — marriages, engagements, promises to “always be there.” She was adamant about finding her soulmate, the person she would settle down and spend her life with. Sadly, none of the relationships worked out, and quite a few ended in a serious heartbreak. As she realized later, the problem was that she always rushed into her relationships, diving into them headfirst and leaving common sense behind. Not used to being alone, Lopez was constantly looking for another person to be with. She was ready to love her partners strongly and fiercely in all her relationships, but she never stopped and thought about loving herself first.

You have to learn to be okay on your own before building a healthy relationship with someone else.

The day her two babies were born became a turning point for Jennifer Lopez. It completely redefined the concept of love for her. The love she felt towards her kids was pure and unconditional. It was truly empowering and cathartic.

Though romantic love differs from parental love, it is the same at its very core. Love should be simple, giving, comfortable, unconditional, and, as she realized, most of her past relationships did not fit this description. The power of parental love pushed her towards yet another epiphany — she didn’t really know how to love and put herself first. Through all her experiences and relationships, she always made compromises, giving away all of herself without expecting much in return. For some unfathomable reason, she never fully embraced her light and greatness. Ultimately, she was relying on others to give her the love that she wasn’t giving herself.

Basing your feeling of self-worth on someone else’s validation is a damaging behavioral pattern.

Everyone is always talking about the importance of loving yourself first. But what does loving yourself truly mean? Nobody teaches you that. As Jennifer Lopez realized over the course of her life, loving yourself is the key to true happiness. For different people, it can mean different things. Still, in general, it is all about making good decisions for yourself, whether it’s simply lying down and reading a good book or finally breaking off an unhappy marriage.

When you fill yourself with self-love, you can do absolutely everything. Your happiness becomes yours alone.

Did you know? Jennifer Lopez has more than 60 songs about love or with the word “love” in the title.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
Life is a process of constant learning. You make choices, sometimes — mistakes, and learn from your experiences and the experiences of those around you. Perhaps, this is the beauty of life — there are multitudes of small epiphanies every step along the way, and each teaches something and gives the tools to become better and stronger.

Changing patterns and thought processes is not an easy job; something as significant as this doesn’t happen overnight; it takes time and lots of practice. When you’re learning to love yourself after years of neglecting your own needs and happiness, you have to try and try and try again until it becomes second nature, a mere reflex.

It also helps to remember that your imperfections are precisely what makes you so human. Even a flawless superstar like Jennifer Lopez makes mistakes, fails, and has to pick herself up to keep going constantly. Her journey to the top wasn’t an easy one. It was tumultuous, sometimes chaotic, and even scary, but it gave her the most precious gift of all. Through her struggles, she discovered the power of self-love and self-acceptance, which is the very fuel of happiness.

Accept the messy parts of your life, own your story, and work towards becoming a better version of yourself. Though the journey towards healing might be dark and painful, the view on the other side is worth the trouble.

Sometimes you have to explore the darkness to get to the light and get back to who you are. ~ Jennifer Lopez

Try this

Write out the things that currently make you happy and the things that are bringing you down. Visualizing this can help you focus on the positives and diminish the power of the negative influences in your life.

Explore what loving yourself means for you specifically — perhaps reading a good book or slowing down a bit? Whatever it is, try to put yourself first and do it!

Read it till the end?
